summary: Completed comprehensive testing of In-House Document Signing System. Backend: 17/17 tests passed. Frontend: All pages and features working correctly after fixing API URL prefix issue. Full signing flow successfully tested end-to-end.
passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/documents/templates - returns list of templates (2 templates)
Backend: GET /api/documents/templates/{id} - returns specific template
Backend: GET /api/documents/templates?include_inactive=true - includes inactive templates
Backend: POST /api/documents/templates - creates new text template
Backend: POST /api/documents/templates - validation rejects invalid template_type
Backend: POST /api/documents/templates - validation rejects text template without content
Backend: GET /api/documents/templates/{invalid_id} - returns 404
Backend: Templates endpoint requires authentication
Backend: POST /api/documents/send - sends document for signing
Backend: POST /api/documents/send - validation rejects invalid template_id (404)
Backend: POST /api/documents/send - validation rejects invalid member_id (404)
Backend: GET /api/documents/requests - returns signing requests list
Backend: GET /api/documents/requests?member_id={id} - filters by member
Backend: GET /api/documents/requests?status=pending - filters by status
Backend: GET /api/documents/sign/{invalid_token} - returns 404 (public endpoint)
Backend: POST /api/documents/sign/{invalid_token}/submit - returns 404
Backend: Public signing endpoint does not require authentication
Frontend: Document Templates page loads correctly
Frontend: Document Templates page shows 2 templates (Financial Hardship, TEST_Template)
Frontend: Create Template button present and functional
Frontend: View, Edit, Deactivate buttons on template cards
Frontend: Show Inactive toggle button present
Frontend: SignDocument page displays error for invalid token
Frontend: SignDocument page loads with valid signing token
Frontend: Document content (Financial Hardship Application) displayed
Frontend: Type Signature / Draw Signature toggle buttons present
Frontend: Full Legal Name input pre-filled with recipient name
Frontend: Signature Preview shows typed name in italic font
Frontend: Consent checkbox with legal text present
Frontend: Sign Document button enables after consent
Frontend: Full signing flow completed successfully
Frontend: Success page displayed after signing ('Document Signed!')
Database: Signing request status updated to 'signed'
Database: Audit trail recorded (signer name, IP, consent, signature type)
Database: Signature record created in signatures collection
critical_code_review_comments: FIXED: API URL prefix issue in DocumentTemplates.js and SignDocument.js - changed from `process.env.REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL` to `${process.env.REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL}/api`
seed_data_creation: Created TEST_Template during backend testing. Used existing 'Financial Hardship Application' template for full flow testing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Document Signing System fully functional. Backend endpoints: GET/POST /api/documents/templates, POST /api/documents/send, GET /api/documents/requests, GET /api/documents/sign/{token} (public), POST /api/documents/sign/{token}/submit (public). Frontend pages: /document-templates (admin), /sign/:signingToken (public). Full signing flow tested: send document -> access signing page -> review content -> agree to consent -> submit signature -> success. Email sending may not work in preview environment (SMTP config). Signing token is stored in MongoDB signing_requests collection but not exposed via API for security.
